tldr: Switching from Hyprland to Rectangle means moving from a Linux Wayland compositor to a macOS window manager — you change OS and keep only the spirit of keyboard-driven window control.
why_switch: You are moving your daily driver to macOS
Rectangle gives keyboard and drag-to-snap window control without OS-level config
Free and open source, with no telemetry
Optional Pro tier for cascade and custom grids
what_to_export: Backup of dotfiles for future Linux use
export_method: Open hyprland.conf and copy out the bind = lines plus any windowrule and monitor entries. Translate each keybind into the action you want on macOS (move to half, quarter, next display, etc.). There is no machine-readable migration path between Hyprland and Rectangle — treat the export as a human-readable shortcut sheet you'll use to configure Rectangle.
import_method: Install Rectangle on macOS via the official site or Homebrew. Open Preferences and rebind shortcuts to match your Hyprland muscle memory as closely as possible. Configure drag-to-snap zones, then grant the Accessibility permission so Rectangle can move windows. For workflows Rectangle cannot match (true tiling, scripted layouts), evaluate Yabai or Amethyst as complements per their official docs.

concept_mapping.note: Map action by action; semantics differ
Rectangle does not manage Spaces directly
Rectangle is snap-based, not true tiling
Use third-party tools (Hammerspoon) for per-app rules

gotchas: Rectangle is macOS-only — this requires switching OS, not just apps
True tiling behavior is not available; consider Yabai for stricter tiling
Accessibility permission must be granted on first launch
Workspace concepts differ significantly between Hyprland and macOS Spaces
Plugin-based Hyprland workflows do not have one-to-one replacements
Custom grids and cascade need Rectangle Pro — verify on the official site

email.subject: Moving from Hyprland to Rectangle on macOS
email.body:
Hey,

I'm switching my main machine to macOS and replacing my Hyprland setup with Rectangle for window management. Rectangle covers keyboard snapping and drag zones, which gets me most of what I used Hyprland for day to day.

I'm keeping the hyprland.conf in dotfiles for any future Linux box. For workflows Rectangle can't match (true tiling, scripted layouts), I'll evaluate Yabai as a follow-up.

Thanks
